Patch Set 5:

Ping Aurelien, so that we can start working on porting this fix to other branches.

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> Sebastien, Bruno, is there some reproducer that we could add as a regression test for this without the need for a full-scale test?

The problem this patch is addressing is an overflow of init_user_ns refcount, and an underflow of new_cred->user_ns refcount. But I do not know any possibility of checking those refcounts outside of the kernel.
Maybe Bruno or Aurelien have an idea?

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> Ping Aurelien, so that we can start working on porting this fix to other branches.

Well, Aurelien is in vacation until Sept 2nd ...

Patch Set 5:

Just to confirm that I am still unable to reproduce when running with patch-set #5 ;-)

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> > Patch Set 4:
> > 
> > Sebastien, Bruno, is there some reproducer that we could add as a regression test for this without the need for a full-scale test?
> 
> The problem this patch is addressing is an overflow of init_user_ns refcount, and an underflow of new_cred->user_ns refcount. But I do not know any possibility of checking those refcounts outside of the kernel.
> Maybe Bruno or Aurelien have an idea?

You better mean an underflow of init_user_ns refcount and an overflow of new_cred->user_ns refcount, right ?!

Yes, I know only to access user_namespaces inside Kernel memory using crash tool.

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> You better mean an underflow of init_user_ns refcount and an overflow of new_cred->user_ns refcount, right ?!
> 

Correct!

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> Sebastien, Bruno, is there some reproducer that we could add as a regression test for this without the need for a full-scale test?

We have only been able to reproduce this problem when running fio inside our containers infrastructures.
So, there should be a way using "unshare -U -r ..."

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> > Patch Set 4:
> > 
> > Sebastien, Bruno, is there some reproducer that we could add as a regression test for this without the need for a full-scale test?
> 
> We have only been able to reproduce this problem when running fio inside our containers infrastructures.
> So, there should be a way using "unshare -U -r ..."

unshare is what I used for my testing, so indeed this is a possibility for a reproducer. But the problem is to check that refcounts on the user namespaces are correct or not. And running IOs for an arbitrary amount of time and then just see if the client crashed or not is not a reliable non-regression test.

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> > Patch Set 5:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 4:
> > > 
> > > Sebastien, Bruno, is there some reproducer that we could add as a regression test for this without the need for a full-scale test?
> > 
> > The problem this patch is addressing is an overflow of init_user_ns refcount, and an underflow of new_cred->user_ns refcount. But I do not know any possibility of checking those refcounts outside of the kernel.
> > Maybe Bruno or Aurelien have an idea?
> 
> You better mean an underflow of init_user_ns refcount and an overflow of new_cred->user_ns refcount, right ?!
> 
> Yes, I know only to access user_namespaces inside Kernel memory using crash tool.

hmmm, I forgot to indicate that bpftrace could be also used to monitor user namespaces refcounts incs and decs, but then the tricky part is to be able to identify each of them ...

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

I was thinking exactly of running something like start/stop container in a minimal loop, long enough that the underflow would happen (e.g. a few hundred or thousand iterations), maybe under SLOW=yes if it takes more than 5 minutes to reproduce.

For now I'm content that Bruno was able to test manually to confirm this patch is working, so that the fix can move forward, but it would be better to avoid hitting it again in the future.
Patch Set 5: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as e2156e69cc87850cac455c1e16776bbd64cacf10 by Oleg Drokin

commit.subject: LU-18145 gss: fix key unlink for regular user
commit.message:
LU-18145 gss: fix key unlink for regular user

In case a key is unlinked for a regular user, it can be necessary to
switch credentials to find a valid keyring for that user, which
implies changing the user namespace as well. This needs a reference
drop on the original namespace, as a ref was taken by prepare_creds(),
and also to take a reference on the newly set namespace, as a ref will
be dropped when put_cred() is finally called.

Because of the user switch, calling get_user_keyring() also adds one
more ref on the user keyring, for kernel implementation based on user
keyring pinned from the user_struct struct. This extra ref needs to be
dropped, but just for this specific case.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Test-Parameters: testgroup=review-dne-selinux-ssk-part-1
Test-Parameters: testgroup=review-dne-selinux-ssk-part-2
Test-Parameters: kerberos=true testlist=sanity-krb5
Fixes: bc740feeaa ("LU-17714 gss: support revoked session keyring")
Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
